title: GROUP_LEAVE get_group check is redundant with is_group_member
description: GROUP_LEAVE in ws.rs first calls get_group (to check group existence), then calls is_group_member. But is_group_member uses COUNT(*) on group_members — if the group doesn't exist, the count is 0 and is_group_member returns false, already producing NOT_A_MEMBER. The get_group call is redundant. It forces two DB round-trips where one suffices, and the error message for a nonexistent group vs a non-member group is different (GROUP_NOT_FOUND vs NOT_A_MEMBER) — which leaks whether the group exists at all. Consider collapsing to a single membership check with a unified NOT_A_MEMBER error.
